summary: Justin Brennan teaches how to buy 10-30 unit apartment buildings with minimal capital ($20K out of pocket) using syndication and OPM (other people's money). He breaks down a real 31-unit case study showing how to structure deals with 70% debt financing, leverage acquisition fees, and execute multiple exits including refinancing and cash-out strategies. The video targets newer investors, emphasizing that expertise is not required—only skill-building and action.
takeaways: Focus on 10-30 unit deals as a newbie (easier than 50+ units with mom-and-pop sellers/brokers)
Syndication structure: You bring 10% of capital (~$50K), control 40% of deal; OPM brings 90% (~$450K), gets 60%
Acquisition fee: 2-3% of purchase price, paid within days of close, built into closing costs (reduces your true out-of-pocket to $20K on a $50K investment)
31-unit case study: $1.525M purchase + $400K renovation + $225K closing = $2.15M all-in; 70% LTV loan = $1.505M; your $64K in, $45K acquisition fee out = $19K net out-of-pocket
Pre-market deals: 80% of multifamily deals sell to brokers' 25-50 investor friends before hitting websites (leftovers are only 20% of market)
3-4 deals in 10-30 unit range generate $10-20K monthly passive income; plan 2-3 years to scale
Don't overlever: Use 70% debt + 30% equity + fixed-rate debt; cash-flowing assets are inflation-proof regardless of market conditions

skill_candidates.change: Learn to calculate acquisition fees (2-3% of purchase price) and true out-of-pocket capital after fee recovery to accurately model deal returns
skill_candidates.evidence: Brennan shows the $50K investment minus $30K acquisition fee = $20K true cost; this fee structure is standard and built into closing costs, reducing capital requirement by 40-60%

skill_candidates.change: Shift from hunting public listings to accessing pre-market deals via direct broker relationships; understand that 80% of deals never hit websites
skill_candidates.evidence: Brennan explains the 'cowgirl and cowboys club' dynamic: brokers make 5x more calls to sellers than direct investors; they pre-market deals to 25-50 investors first, then move to websites as leftovers only if unsold

skill_candidates.change: Dedicate 5-10 hours per week initially (2 hours/night after day job) to find, analyze, and structure deals; don't wait for perfect conditions
skill_candidates.evidence: Brennan shares his own story: worked as real estate agent 10 hours/day, then spent 2 hours/night on deal analysis until momentum built; emphasizes 'the key is not giving up' and following a 90-day action plan consistently

skill_candidates.change: Master the 10-30 unit underwriting model with 70% LTV financing; validate that deals cash flow from day one with fixed-rate debt before committing capital
skill_candidates.evidence: Brennan's case study: 31-unit refinanced from $1.525M to $3.6M value in 24 months; pulled $2.5M cash-out after paying off original $1.505M loan and returning investor equity; model requires NOI analysis to justify refi assumptions

title: How To Buy Your First Apartment Building Fast!
